'll hear what what they are advising us to do thank you gentlemen for joining us this evening thank you councilman can everyone see the screen yes thank you michael good evening everyone for those of you who might not know me my name is michael fonzino the director of the west orange health department i'd like to thank council president for giving me the opportunity to provide some brief kova 19 updates this evening so currently the township is experiencing a significant increase in covet 19 cases as is the county state and the country as a whole thankfully hospitalizations and deaths remain low township continues to provide weekly covit statistics on our website and through our social media outlets through the mayor presides weekly coven 19 updates so you can find these on our website and if you're on facebook or twitter they're also posted there so vaccination and testing sites are operational at the county

our primary site is the former kmart building located at 235 prospect avenue west orange there's various different hours for specific age groups for vaccinations ages five and up all vaccinations can be scheduled online at www.essexcova.org or by calling eight four 973-877-8450 six they're currently not taking any walk-ins the only walk-ins they're taking is for testing which is conducted at all three of the vaccination sites in addition to kmart essex county college and the former sears building in the livingston mall are the other two county sites but testing is done monday through saturday from 9 30 a.m to 6 30 p.m again no no appointments are required for testing there are additional testing sites and we have a list on our website at westorange.org coronavirus and then there's also a testing page on the covid new jersey hub where 289 00:11:

you can search out other testing sites in addition to what the county is offering booster vaccinations we're they're finding that vaccinations start waning at six months for both moderna and pfizer and at two months for j and j boosters are highly recommended at these intervals to provide continued protection as council president stated the cdc and new jersey department of health shortened the recommended isolation and quarantine period for the general public now this this information is as of today and could change at any time but i just want to emphasize that these recommend recommendations are for the general population and there's other guidelines that are different for schools healthcare personnel and other ancillary professions again these are the current guidelines and are subject to change by cdc and njdoh at any time since this situation is very fluid i'll just kind of go through this quickly i'm not going to read it word for w

ord but currently if you test positive for covin 19 regardless of your vaccination status you must isolate which means stay home for five days if you have no symptoms or your symptoms are improving after five days you can leave your home but continue to wear masks when you're around others for an additional five days and if you have a fever you need to continue to stay home until your fever resolves without any fever reducing medications for exposure if you've been exposed to a confirmed case of coven 19 quarantining is as follows if you've been boosted completed the primary series of fisa or mederena vaccine within the last six months or completed a primary series of j and j within the last two months you you would need to wear a mask around others for 10 days test on day 5 if possible really testing should be done between day 5 and seven and if you start to develop symptoms get tested and stay home until

-> 00:15:27,920 of others in the setting is unknown and where an individual may be immune compromised or increased risk for severe disease you want to make sure that you correctly wear a face covering or mask making sure one you can breathe through it make sure your nose and your mouth are covered that the the mask fits snugly against your face and you want to pick a mask with two or more layers of washable breathable fabric or a surgical type mask you want to be sure to wash cloth cloth masks after each use and dispose of single use masks if you're using them so in just a quick summary on what the west orange health department has been doing over the past year and continues to do the health department has a partnership with the montclair state university school of nursing to provide vaccinations to our homebound residents long-term care facilities senior apartment buildings and our schools in addition the health department and montclair state university are prov

iding pop-up mobile vaccination clinics in neighborhoods with vulnerable populations west orange health department continues to conduct daily covet 19 case investigations and contact tracing for the general population schools and long-term care facilities west orange health department continues to update messaging and guidelines on our website and social media outlets as information changes with that if there's any questions my information is below i could be reached via email or my telephone number is also below you can feel free to reach out to me at any time this powerpoint presentation will also be posted on the township website for those who may want to refer to it later on thank you very much you very much michael that or director fanzina we do appreciate that best defense as you said vaccinations i appreciate the booster information about how you said the vaccinations may be waning and that we should continue to get boo

0:22:40,320 you know the best thing to use at this point okay my last question too some of the towns around us have moved to an indoor mass mandate meaning you must wear a mask indoors at any of the properties you know businesses or public spaces within their township is that something that we should be considering here what we yeah mayor go ahead do you want to yeah so obviously michael and nick can weigh in for from their perspectives but and look we've had a lot of communication from members of the public in the business community on both sides of the issue so as in many decisions we've been forced to make over these last two years it's hard to say if there's a real clear right answer and that's the challenge but despite what some of our neighboring towns have done we're not prepared to move in that direction yet and and first of all it's it's a few practical reasons 603 00:23:42,4

first and most importantly everyone should be wearing a mask so the fact that we need to mandate it is kind of a little frustrating that we're two years into this and people still feel with a thousand cases last week that they shouldn't wear a mask but but that aside we don't have we don't have the ability to police that and i certainly don't want to put our business owners in a position where they have to police it so i recognize that it sends an important symbolic message but from practically speaking it's it's difficult to enforce and i don't want to create a problem for business owners where they're forced to get confrontational with people business owners and many have they have the ability to mandate it themselves there's several businesses in town that have signs on their door don't come in without a mask and they still have that right but but just a blanket mandate from the town sends the right mess

age but not really something that's easy to enforce and and i think when you look at the numbers over the last two weeks though we don't want our businesses to suffer more than they have truth is we probably shouldn't be going into businesses we should stay at home wherever possible and other than maybe getting required food items at shoprite or whole foods you know we should be limiting where we're going anyway so i know there's been a handful of people who've reached out and quite honestly a handful of people who reached out and urged us not to too so tough decision but our decision is that we're not we're not prepared to do that we think people should be wearing the mask we encourage them to wear the mask but a mandate does a little bit more than than you know been posting it because we have no means of governing it nor nor should business owners be in a position to to have to fight with people if they don't really want to wear a mask so we decided no

t to whether opinion changes in the next week or two we'll see but as of right now we're not prepared to thank you mayor yeah i i would encourage us to at least consider it i you know i know it's a tough decision i don't envy your position no matter what you do in this kind of a situation there's going to be somebody to complain and something is going to go wrong either way so i i completely get it but i do think even though it's a symbolic gesture i think it would go a long way toward encouraging people to wear masks more frequently in indoor spaces you know i'm thinking particularly of the shoprite type places where you have large gatherings and some people just don't want to wear them and you know it can be it can certain in some cases it can be deadly thank you that's it for me council president mr mayor mr fonzino thank you guys you're welcome councilman councilwoman casolino thank you council president and thank you director and our directors for coming on this evening always so

ng outdating anti-loitering ordinances in july we adopted an ordinance requiring police officers to always wear operational body cameras adopted ordinances prohibiting the use of rubber bullets or tear gas adopted an ordinance clearly defining directing directives on the use of force and reporting the use of force we entered into an agreement with a nationally recognized diversity control consultant trainer and speaker dr david jones who will also be joining us this evening to provide implicit bias training for all employees and elected officials and we entered into this pilot program that is my lead-in for the chief with essex and mrs county mental health association to assist police on responding to call where that may be necessary to have a social worker alongside so good evening chief thank you for joining us good evening thank you for having me golf script for a minute and thank councilwoman to brown for leadership over the past year it's been 1219 00:47

a pleasure working with you as the council president and to welcome susan to the presidency once again i've had a years of experience with working with you in the past and look forward to the the next year as well and special thanks and welcoming to my good friend john gross who i think will has always served the town in the past admirably and i think we'll continue to do so and i learn from john every day i've been doing this a long time and sometimes you know you really think you know it all and then you sit with john you find out what you don't know and he's he's a good friend and he's always there for me so i appreciate that i'd welcome him so back to script back in 19 from july of 19 to july of 20 we found that we had 30 incidents 393 mental health calls that re resulted in 30 incidents of use of force now the way use of force is tracked in new jersey is every officer that's involved has to submit a report so those 30 incidents resulted in 83 1248 00:48:1

different filings from 83 officers and when advanced media did their presentation to the public on use of force that didn't play very well for many municipalities and we were also one of them of those 83 uses of force those people that were involved with mental health crisis were sometimes forced into ambulances and taken to facilities where they were better served rather than penal institutions but nonetheless their uses of force and and new jersey advanced media did what they did with that so we we looked at it and we had concerns and i went to my my go-to man richard trank who knows somebody somewhere for everything so richard introduced us to bob davidson at the mental health associates of essex morris and we undertook a pilot program that was the first in the state and there's really not that many in the nation every every day when i read the online periodicals for what's going on policing around the country i see another major

and theater goers as well as west orange residents looking for a place to gather and enjoy live entertainment covent has kept people from enjoying live entertainment for so long and that's what has made the west orange outdoor venue a highlight this summer for so many people both on stage and off stage it was great to be able to go watch the performances but if you speak to any of the performers they will tell you it was equally rewarding for them to be able to get back on stage and perform for us in 2021 we opened the theater early to allow for the high schools to use the space for their spring musicals and concerts because their auditoriums were closed to public audiences we also produced a free admission original children's musical a pride month play called this is our story in june with proceeds that benefited the rain foundation at that particular event art students from the high school designed and hand painted a canvas mural on that big retaining wall up at aus

form in october and they will likely close out our season again we are honored very honored to produce a one night event in june jelani remy the back home series is what we are calling it jelani has a very strong tie he's the guy right here in that nice velvet shirt he has very strong ties to auspak having performed on stage as a young boy and now with several broadway credits on his resume he continues to feel a connection to our outdoor beautiful theater he directed smokey joe's cafe last year and he helped us produce motown during covet summer which was a very needed event and very well received and the only event so far where we've had to turn audience members away we will also announce our main stage broadway musical production shortly we can't announce that until we officially have the licensing from licensing companies our original musical this year is called goblins and gates and it is written and composed by an essex county resident and it will be presented in mid-august and

this year we are proud excited a little bit nervous but super excited to bring back what we've all been waiting for which is the annual jazz fest people are anxious and they would like it back and we want it back in west orange because it's in montclair it's in maplewood and it needs to be in west orange these plans are expensive pvp has worked very hard to manage programming without losing money pvp doesn't need to make money but pvp would prefer not losing five cents we find ourselves without the 2021 fundraising opportunities to maintain the upcoming budget we're kind of hoping that the township will help us a little bit maybe sponsor some of the programming so that we can continue to provide a well-rounded lineup of shows and events early on when we first signed on to the agreement with the township we were aware that the township was not going to provide programming money we were aware of it we were told flat out and we figured out how to make it 304 0

we are a small board of volunteers we do much of the work ourselves but we need to hire skilled lighting and audio technicians designers performing artists to make it all work we did something smart and we recently elected four new board members to help us continue our goals for pvp at us back one of them may be new to our board but she is certainly not new to the auspac stage a resident of west orange and an auspac singer since the very very very beginning of us back lynette sheared will will spearhead our plans to bring jazz festival back to west orange with lynette's energy background and jazz music and her resources we're confident that jazz fest will be back on the map in 2022 lynette would like to say hello and talk to you of it so may i have lynette be off mute welcome hello hello hello thank you thank you thank you happy black history month thank you council for giving me the opportunity as camille mentioned for

those of you who are not aware my name is lynette sheard i have i'm gonna tell you a little story i've been a west orange resident for over 30 years now i was originally born in newark new jersey affectionately known as nook let me tell you a story my ex-husband and i brought a home in the gregory section my three daughters thrived it was great we went we loved west orange we went to the park we went to the pool we went to the zoo we went to school concerts movies skating hiking i love the school system and the music program oh my gosh is top-notch each one of them played an instrument were in the band and even the marching band the award-winning marching band fast forward 10 years i'm divorced single mom of three and had to move my home went into foreclosure and i lost my job due to reorganization i quickly transitioned to telecom sales because that's what we all do when you make a whole lot of money real quick at the time and was able to find an apartment in west orange on franklin avenue a

block from washington school and across from colgate and the town pool which we that was our activity every every day actually with my new job unfortunately i worked all the time to make ends meet and my landlord was very kind there were times i was late but i always paid extra when i could and i took care of the property fun fact i recently purchased this multi-family home in december after living here as a tenant for 25 years okay back to it we were struggling to find some peace some joy some fun and and the big thing connection i was a single mom working around the clock on international conference calls and schmoozing interviewing sales no schmoozing is exhausting after a while i could buy what we needed but i was losing time joy and connection with my daughters they sat me down and said to me these are kids you look sad mommy how are we supposed to be happy if you're not they actually said that to me they had an intervention w

ith me so i prayed as i do and i meditated and decided to quit my high-powered stress job in sales and decided to sing and serve i love to sing and i love to serve i was not prepared but i knew i needed to seek happiness as my daughters intuitively advised i needed to find a passion and pursue it i bought a karaoke machine from best buy on route 10 and i learned one song a day and we would go to nursing homes and senior citizens complexes me and my daughters and sing for the residents i call them seasoned citizens they like that they were honest about what i sang if they hated the song they said shut up sing something else i learned a lot my daughters watch me sing laugh smile find joy while they painted the resident's fingernails and made art for their rooms or their apartments i offered free entertainment and it changed our lives we were doing something together that inspired and provided a cost-efficient emotional outlet and a connection

designed saint marks his grandson about 80 years later designed holy trinity and it has all the aspects of a tudor revival church buttresses half timber framing but underlying its white exterior is an interesting story very relevant to west orange which is that this church was built by the parishioners by the community bloc literally blocked by block they used hollow terracotta blocks and laid them up and they designed the building that way so they could do it with their own volunteer labor which i think is an extraordinary story that they were doing this in 1907 in order to create a a a place for the tory corner neighborhood it has a lovely interior i i'm sure many of you have been in there but it's if you haven't it's really it's really worth going in to see the the workmanship on the stained glass windows and the property itself from a tax parcel point of view has the c

pages.text: so good evening everyone welcome to our march 1st town council meeting and the start of women's history month on that note i have invited our township and engineer lenny lapore to introduce the new assistant engineer so we are very pleased to see her via zoom and hope to meet her in person soon and and to welcome her to west orange already exchanged emails so we look forward to meeting you in person and the good news is i'm hearing from dpw that lenny is actually delegating work so that is a great news those age beth welcome welcome to west orange council president i have to do the opening statement yes sorry okay this is to inform the general public that this meeting is being held in compliance with section 5 of the open public meetings act chapter 231 public law 1975. the annual notice was emailed to the star ledger and filed in the township clerk's office on november 24th 2021 and published in the

three weeks ago at the beginning of february she is certainly knowledgeable educated knowledgeable knows how to apply various principles of engineering and her experience to west orange doesn't necessarily know the ins and outs of the town but she is learning very quickly we're a fairly sizable town so it's not going to come overnight it's it's going to take some time but i'm i'm very pleased that that she is here as you said i am delegating her work because now i have somebody to delegate work to and if you have any questions for me or her she's here so i'll introduce you to zazabeth carbello carbella thank you very much very impressive resume thank you welcome thank you would you like to say a few words sure i am very excited about this opportunity i definitely believe that it's gonna make good use of my education and experience to handle the many challenges of this position

or jenny cunningham crawley and she was influential in the field of journalism she became the first woman reporter employed by a metropolitan newspaper and this was in new york to write in the office on a daily basis and she served as a regular correspondent for out of town daily papers and to manage a women's department of the newspaper and she also taught journalism so and the origins of how the women's club started were because of this woman so she was the founding president and she this club was really formed out of anger because back in march of 1868 the new york press club held a dinner in honor of charles dickens and at the end of his second reading tour in the united states so men and even with even slight literary connections wanted tickets for this event and so did two of the best known and most quoted women writers of the day one of them was jenny cunningham crawley and the other was sarah willis parton despite the fact that mrs crawley's

0:13:43,519 husband was on the executive committee of the club the male membership was outraged that she should dare to make this a promiscuous social occasion they had refused to admit women so jenny's response to this sexist treatment re received from the influential male organization was to found a club cirrhosis which really became the first women's club of note so her aim was to unite women to reach their political or excuse me their potential and cultivate their place in society so the women's club started right here in our in our backyard there was a club in westar in not west orange in orange that was one of the original clubs that was founded in 1872 and for those of you who may not know the women's club was really influential in getting women on school boards they also started douglas college down at rutgers which was one of the first colleges for women in new jersey and did a lot of work with douglas bringing in science programs and other 406 00:

pages.text: this is to inform the general public that this meeting is being held in compliance with section 5 of the open public meetings act chapter 231 public law 1975. the annual notice was emailed to the star ledger and filed in the township clerk's office on november 24 2021 and published in the west orange chronicle on december 2nd 2021. councilwoman castellino present councilwoman matue brown present councilman rutherford here councilwoman williams president council president mccartney here thank you welcome all good evening welcome to our tuesday march 15 2022 town council meeting i wanted to start with the fact that it's 20 days into the invasion of ukraine and let's continue to keep the millions of people being evacuated in our prayers i was really pleased to see that our fire department is donating 10 sets of turnout gear it's quite a bit of equipment and needed equipment we found out i was trying to work with the hrc 33 00:01:18

the human relations commission when i saw arrow adding our resolution to the council agenda showing our support and i wanted to do more because i know i just know west orange and how people in west orange would want to contribute in some way so there is a maplewood ukrainian church orthodox ukrainian church in maplewood i contacted a representative there they have been inundated with the supplies that they were looking for first aid supplies diapers and personal hygiene items but now what they need are the funding to ship the items the same thing with the church in clifton as well so rabbi tobin also on the human relations commission he is merging with a synagogue in verona there is a planned service of prayer and unity on sunday march 27th at five o'clock and he's also working with the jewish federation greater metro west working with the ukrainian american cultural center which is located in whippany so we look forward to finding out

more information on ways that the township can try and help everyone in our prayers i wanted to say thank you to the town employees who have navigated residents through covid now the town hall is open all offices functioned all through covet and now that the offices are open to the public just a hearty welcome to all the employees that that were working town hall and congratulations to six police officers we have lieutenant dennis mccole here we go sergeant jose alvarez sergeant christopher polizzano sergeant jeffrey james jr sergeant michael del plato and sergeant mark fouriers so congratulations gentlemen and we used are in our prayers as well and we hope that you continue to stay safe safe and well thank you so this evening still being west orange i'm sorry still being women's history month i wanted to recognize mark and julie orenstein jul oh here they are so shopkeepers ice cre

scratch it it feels like relief ticks are the relief releasing the tension having ts while being a student can be exhausting children will sometimes try to suppress their texts which is which makes it hard to focus on what the teacher is teaching and it's physically exhausting as well ts is chronic and there's no cure i feel fortunate enough to have outgrown of many of my obvious tics i used to have such as yelling shuffling my feet and moving my neck now i have sub soul ticks such as taking an extra breath before i speak or small facial movements most people don't even notice them anymore but i still feel them i have used sports and started physically being physically active as a way to help cope with my ts i will be a senior at west orange high school in the next year and i'm grateful for my family coaches and teachers who have supported me along the way i'm here because educating i'm here because educating

0 situations he told me today was a busy day anthony's bedside manner put us all to shame dedicated to improving emt knowledge and skills throughout the department highly motivated to help anyone in need anthony is always being fought over when picking partners and always caring for not only his patients but for his team members so we are very proud and honored to recognize anthony williams of the west orange fire department for being selected as the emt of the year 2020 22 and we wish him continued success congratulations thank you for your service as a navy vet as well i also wanted to recognize the girls softball champions you ate you know here i thought school was out and they wouldn't be available i understand the day after school was out they already had game they already had practice so i'm hoping within the next our next council meeting hopefully we can get them together that's great what's next our co

pages.text: so welcome welcome to our august 9 2022 town council meeting madam clerk please this is to inform the general public that this meeting is being held in compliance with section 5 of the open public meetings act chapter 231 public law in 1975. the annual notice was emailed to the star ledger and filed in the township clerk's office on november 24th 2021 and published in the west orange chronicle on december 2nd 2021. councilwoman casolino present councilman rutherford here councilwoman williams present council president mccartney present thank you madam clerk so the first thing on our conference agenda is this beautiful proclamation to jimmy buffs celebrating 90 years if there's anyone here from the downtown alliance i know our executive director here is here i know people are on zoom please come up no jimmy buffs is celebrating its 90th anniversary originally of 14th avenue and 19th street in newark new jersey is where the hot dog w

ith the italian twist was first concocted by the founder james bluff rasiope in 1932 and whereas grandpa got his nickname from his bluffing style at the car table where he affectionately was called jimmy bluff translation from italian to english came up with the nickname buff in the twenties and thirties everyone had a nickname and hence james became known as jimmy buff whereas the sandwich was first served as a treat by the rashiops for close friends and relatives it went over so big with them it became the center of their menu and ultimately a customer favorite the fresh onions peppers and potatoes combined with a hot dog is what allows the specialty its claim to originality and whereas today over 90 years later the italian hot dog and its nearest relative the italian sausage are the staple fare at jimmy buffs and take turns out selling each other today they cook their italian hot dogs and sausages and cholesterol-free soybean oil just like grandpa buff did in 1932. the tradition lives on toda

y in west orange and kenilworth both locations are run by james braziopi's two great grandsons thomas rasiope runs the west orange location and michael lloyd runs the kennel with kenilworth location whereas jimmy buffs has survived many obstacles in the 90 years but the pandemic was the toughest but it did force them into a local delivery business we also ship our sandwiches nationwide through a company called gold belly which ships iconic foods all over the country also 2022 marks the third year they are doing on-site backyard parties so now therefore on behalf of mayor parisi and my town council colleagues and community we do hereby congratulate jimmy buffs on their 90th anniversary and their 62nd year in our community and extend our warmest best best wishes for long and continued success i want to get in the picture sure did you want to say something first off i want to thank the town list orange for giving us this proclamation it's been

our honor to be here for the last 62 years i want to thank my grandfather for having the great idea to come to west orange from newark my father for keeping it going and the rest of our family for all our support and can't wait for the next 60 years so i have one for you guys to take home one for the store one for the hello world thank you guys so much and it's tuesday beautiful hey meg good night all thank you so much thank you we have are you here from the west orange women's club west orange women's club no okay so next on our conference agenda is the green ribbon week presented by miss olivia raymond student ambassador from the grassroots community foundation on mental health here's our presentation olivia can you hear me yes can you hear me 129 00:07:26,960

yes okay the way this is going to work the the audience can see your presentation can you see your presentation yes i can okay you just tell me when you want to to advance to the next slide and i'll take care of it before we begin this presentation i'd just like to introduce miss olivia raymond she is a dynamic individual i have worked with her before she is part of an organization that in 2019 raised funds for haiti and they bought their funding benefit was successful and they bought such things as a goat and a cow among other things of course they were purchased locally in haiti and i and i mentioned this for two reasons that she is a dynamic individual and i wanted to i want her to be taken seriously in this presentation and the other thing is that she is the daughter of our downtown west orange chairman ron raymond okay olivia take it away and tell me when to advance the slides thank you so much for that i

ntroduction and yes i am olivia raymond i am the student ambassador of green german week i am also part of the original group that came up with this idea back in 2017 and i was the ambassador when i was at roosevelt middle school and now i will be a junior this year at west orange high school i'm also on the executive board of the black student union and have been a part of the grassroots community foundation since fifth grade where i was able to do the social acting project which was previously mentioned but i would just like to thank you all for inviting me here today to share with you about green river week and our organization you can go to the next line so the grassroots community foundation is a leadership and training organization with oh you can go to the next slide with an emphasis on public health and social justice which is led and developed by dr janice johnson diaz and our mission is to create a world where all girls grow up to be healthy women and as a part of this we have a flagship

0 program known as super camp which is a summer intensive leadership program that teaches girls of african descent in african about african and african american history public health sociology and political science and we also do activities such as african drumming poetry and dance which it aims to help youth develop body confidence and oration skills and our focus areas are physical sexual and mental health where we aim to increase movement and intake of fruits and vegetables responsible sexual decision making self-efficacy and confidence among youth and adults as well as our understanding of the relationship between health and finance you can go to the next slide so the organization is revolves around four different principles which lays the groundwork of our work that we do which is truth order balance and reciprocity when we say truth we are talking about understanding the basic truths about health and wellness learning skills necessary to make wellness a priority and incorporating those skills

in our everyday lives and practicing them as well as sharing our knowledge and information with friends family and our community and our equation is truth plus order plus balance plus reciprocity equals harmony internal harmony equals joy and external harmony equals justice you can go the next slide so green ribbon week is an initiative that aims to promote positive mental health among youth and our goals are to educate youth about ways to maintain good mental health encourage adults to engage in practices that facilitate dialogue with youth as well as eliminating the stigma by making conversations about mental health just as common as our conversations revolving around physical health and in doing this we are able to move beyond illness to wellness you can go to the next slide the motivation behind green river beach starts back in 2017 when grassroots community foundation and rwj barnabas health collaborated to develop a healthcare fellowship for us to experience with the goal of developing a 247 0

pipeline of future women healthcare leaders where we were able to get a multitude of public health training and professional development as well as learning about policy implementation and experiencing different aspects of the healthcare system and we were able to participate in a fellowship hackathon where we worked with different fellows and interns to strategize solutions for different global health crises and to encompass this whole experience we were asked to create a capstone project where we had to address a gap that we thought was important and unaddressed in the health care system and the group that i was a part of decided to focus on mental health among youth you can go to the next slide so since then green ribbon week has been presented and adopted by west orange high school in collaboration with miss katherine connors and the student council and has won top 10 projects and recognized for 100 participation of the west orange high school student council and has won the youth and students for

peace award in the synergy competition and is continued to be supported by the student council black student union and student assistance counselors and at 21 2021 we are grateful for it to be adopted by the west orange town council you can go to the next slide our other partners include central high school in newark roosevelt middle school redwood elementary school essex county college mastercard td bank hallian which is formerly glaxosmithkline healthcare mandalas hope therapeutics the council for black health rwj barnabas health newark beth israel hospital and municipalities such as the city of plainfield and west orange you can go to the next slide and this support is so important and we're so grateful because it helps us on our journey of addressing the current state of youth mental health and by the statistics you can see that it's so important because one in five teens experience depression before they reach adulthood and over 500 000 kids each year report to the hospital because of self-harm

0 injury and suicide is the second leading cause of death for youth among the ages 10-24 and these greatly affect our educational experiences and outcomes shown by one in three students of mental health needs continue on to post-secondary education and kids who have recorded to have high depression scores also end up having high suspension weights and you can go to the next slide and a key aspect of green ribbon week is moving to a conversation about wellness because the majority of conversations and policies regarding mental health are focused on the treatment of illnesses and disorders and those are very important but we are seeking to broaden the conversation and our perspective when we considered what is necessary to maintain our well-being and taking steps to maintain our wellness can help prevent or minimize the development of illness and these are some of the positive mental health practices that we suggest that people take part in during green ribbon week such as deep breathing which is one of the best ways to lower stres

s in the body unstructured play which helps problem solving skills and emotional intelligence talking out your feelings with someone who is there to support you and listen to maintain a good mental state as well as taking time to go outside maybe to a park or exercise you can sleep to restore your body and mind and it's important to connect with others and develop and maintain the strong relationships you have with people who are there to support and love you you can go the next time so green ribbon week is held on the third week of september from september 19th to september 23rd and on monday we suggest that you take the pledge to practice one or more positive mental health activities each day and start each day this week with at least 60 seconds of deep breathing and on tuesday we suggest that you spend some time playing a game with friends or family outside for at least 20 minutes and we suggest that you wear green every day but especially on wednesday the 21st because that is our day of solidari

re is an Express statutory mandate articulated public policy or other recognized public interest militating toward access the Appellate Division found that all seven factors weighed in favor of non-disclosure the court continued that the personal information does not relate to the core concern of Oprah which is public access to government records moreover the court noted that the Oprah request form did not put citizens on notice that their personal information could be subject to disclosure or advised that citizens can submit a request anonymously with regard to the plaintiff's condition contention that he was entitled to disclosure of the requested private information pursuant to the common law right of access the Appellate Division also found that privacy interests outweighed the plaintiff's right to access when analyzing whether privacy concerns outweigh a common law right to access courts utilize the balancing factors set forth in Ketty versus Rutgers one the records must be common law public document

s two the person seeking access must establish an interest in the subject matter of the material and three the citizens right to access must be balanced against the state's interest in preventing disclosure based on the law I am entitled to my personal records and those that contact me personally from the public should feel comfortable that I will not turn over a a personal record unless there is a public interest in this case there is not thank you council president thank you okay okay Madame Clerk all right we're going to review the consent agenda before you start sure I just want to let you know I think I did let you know actually but I'm going to make it a record here that the executive session is not going to be had tonight because there's nothing further to report Mr beckelman's not here we'll probably have something on at the next meeting but we'll take it a meeting at a time thank you Mr Casey thank you to the public who is exiting we appreciate you coming out this evening good night all
